title: The Body's Refusal
style: kawaii-zen, soft pastels, campus and dorm setting

panels.visual_prompt: Morning light cuts through the dorm curtain in a hard diagonal. Nana's alarm clock reads 10:47 AM. The alarm is off. Her phone shows 6 missed notifications. She lies face-down in the pillow, one arm hanging off the bed. The roommate's bed is already made and empty.
panels.narration: Nana's lecture started at nine. The morning happened without her.
panels.body_anchor: face pressed into pillow, arm dead-weight off the bed

panels.visual_prompt: Close-up of Nana sitting up in bed. Her hair falls in tangled clumps. Her eyes are swollen, half-shut. She holds her own head with both hands, elbows on her knees. The posture is heavy, collapsed. Soft morning light makes the exhaustion visible -- every shadow under her eyes, every crease in her face.
panels.narration: She sits up. Her body moves like it belongs to someone else. Everything weighs more than it did yesterday.
panels.body_anchor: head heavy in hands, eyelids swollen, limbs like sand

panels.visual_prompt: Nana stands at her dorm room door, hand on the knob, wearing yesterday's hoodie. Through a small window in the door, the hallway stretches bright and populated -- students walking, laughing. Nana's reflection in the window glass looks faded, ghostlike. She hesitates.
panels.narration: She grips the door handle. The hallway sounds alive. She stands there for a full minute before turning it.
panels.body_anchor: hand gripping doorknob, feet planted, not moving

panels.visual_prompt: Wide shot of the university dining hall. Bright, noisy, full of students at long tables. Nana stands at the entrance, small in the frame, holding a tray with nothing on it. The crowd flows around her. She looks at the food stations but does not walk toward them.
panels.narration: The dining hall roars with voices and trays and chairs scraping tile. Nana holds an empty tray and stands still.
panels.body_anchor: empty tray gripped with both hands, shoulders high

panels.visual_prompt: Medium shot. Nana turns away from the dining hall. She walks back down a quieter corridor, tray abandoned on a return rack. Her steps are slow, deliberate, like walking through deep water. Other students pass her going the opposite direction. Nobody looks at her.
panels.narration: She puts the tray back. She walks away from the noise. Her legs carry her but she does not feel the floor.
panels.body_anchor: legs heavy, feet dragging, no sensation of floor

panels.visual_prompt: Nana sits on a bench in an empty campus courtyard. Trees with early spring buds surround her. She hunches forward, elbows on knees, staring at the ground. A vending machine hums behind her. She holds a granola bar but has not opened it. Wrappers and petals litter the ground.
panels.narration: She sits outside with a granola bar she bought from the machine. She holds it in both hands. She does not open it.
panels.body_anchor: hands gripping granola bar, hunched forward, stomach closed

panels.visual_prompt: Close-up of Nana's eyes. They are bloodshot, unfocused. The world behind her -- trees, buildings, sky -- is rendered as a soft blur, watercolor edges dissolving. Her vision literally cannot hold the shapes together. Small sparkles of light drift across the panel like visual static.
panels.narration: The campus blurs. Her eyes refuse to focus. She blinks and blinks and the edges of things keep dissolving.
panels.body_anchor: vision blurred, eyes refusing to focus, blinking rapid

panels.visual_prompt: Medium shot, classroom hallway. Nana walks past a lecture hall with the door open. Inside, rows of students take notes. A professor writes on a whiteboard. Nana glances in but keeps walking. Her reflection passes across the glass panel in the door -- translucent, barely there.
panels.narration: She passes the lecture hall. Her seat sits empty in the third row. She keeps walking.

panels.visual_prompt: Back in the dorm room. Afternoon light, warmer now. Nana sits on the floor between the two beds, back against the wall, knees up. Her phone is on the bed above her but she has not picked it up. She stares at the opposite wall. Her hands rest on her knees, limp.
panels.narration: She sits on the floor of her room. The afternoon pours in gold and she does not move toward it or away from it.
panels.body_anchor: back pressed flat against wall, hands limp on knees

panels.visual_prompt: Extreme close-up of Nana's jaw. The masseter muscle is visibly tight, a hard line running from ear to chin. A small vein pulses at her temple. Her lips are pressed together. This is a clinical, almost anatomical close-up -- the body speaking louder than any words.
panels.narration: Her jaw aches. It has ached since midterms. She realizes she cannot remember the last time it did not.
panels.body_anchor: jaw clenched since midterms, masseter tight, temple pulsing

panels.visual_prompt: Medium close-up. Nana raises one hand slowly to her own face. Her fingertips touch her jaw, right at the hinge joint below her ear. Her eyes shift -- not shock, not relief, just the faintest flicker of attention landing on something real. The touch is gentle, exploratory. Afternoon light catches her fingers.
panels.narration: She lifts her hand. Her fingers find the hard knot at the hinge of her jaw. She presses, gently. The muscle holds solid as stone.
panels.body_anchor: fingertips on jaw hinge, pressing gently, discovering tension

panels.visual_prompt: Final panel. Wide shot of Nana on the floor, hand still on her jaw, afternoon light filling the room. The composition is still, almost like a photograph. Nothing has changed in the room. But her eyes are focused now -- looking inward, at the place her fingers found. A single cherry blossom petal drifts past the window outside, barely visible.
panels.narration: Nothing changes. The room stays the same. But her hand stays on her jaw, and for the first time in weeks, Nana notices where she hurts.
panels.body_anchor: hand resting on jaw, eyes focused inward, body beginning to register itself
